root_text.an4.171:1.1: “Kāye vā, bhikkhave, sati kāyasañcetanāhetu uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ.
root_text.an4.171:1.2: Vācāya vā, bhikkhave, sati vacīsañcetanāhetu uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ.
root_text.an4.171:1.3: Mane vā, bhikkhave, sati manosañcetanāhetu uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ avijjāpaccayāva.
root_text.an4.171:2.1: Sāmaṁ vā taṁ, bhikkhave, kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ.
root_text.an4.171:2.2: Pare vāssa taṁ, bhikkhave, kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharonti, yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ.
root_text.an4.171:2.3: Sampajāno vā taṁ, bhikkhave, kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ.
root_text.an4.171:2.4: Asampajāno vā taṁ, bhikkhave, kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ.
root_text.an4.171:3.1: Sāmaṁ vā taṁ, bhikkhave, vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ;
root_text.an4.171:3.2: pare vāssa taṁ, bhikkhave, vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharonti;
root_text.an4.171:3.4: sampajāno vā taṁ, bhikkhave, vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ;
root_text.an4.171:3.5: asampajāno vā taṁ, bhikkhave, vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ.
root_text.an4.171:4.1: Sāmaṁ vā taṁ, bhikkhave, manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ;
root_text.an4.171:4.2: pare vāssa taṁ, bhikkhave, manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharonti, yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ;
root_text.an4.171:4.3: sampajāno vā taṁ, bhikkhave, manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ;
root_text.an4.171:4.4: asampajāno vā taṁ, bhikkhave, manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ.
root_text.an4.171:5.2: avijjāya tveva asesavirāganirodhā so kāyo na hoti yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ, sā vācā na hoti yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ, so mano na hoti yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ,
root_text.an4.171:5.3: khettaṁ taṁ na hoti …pe… vatthu taṁ na hoti …pe… āyatanaṁ taṁ na hoti …pe… adhikaraṇaṁ taṁ na hoti yaṁpaccayāssa taṁ uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhadukkhanti.
root_text.an4.171:6.3: Atthi, bhikkhave, attabhāvapaṭilābho, yasmiṁ attabhāvapaṭilābhe attasañcetanā kamati, no parasañcetanā.
root_text.an4.171:6.4: Atthi, bhikkhave, attabhāvapaṭilābho, yasmiṁ attabhāvapaṭilābhe parasañcetanā kamati, no attasañcetanā.
root_text.an4.171:6.5: Atthi, bhikkhave, attabhāvapaṭilābho, yasmiṁ attabhāvapaṭilābhe attasañcetanā ca kamati parasañcetanā ca.
root_text.an4.171:6.6: Atthi, bhikkhave, attabhāvapaṭilābho, yasmiṁ attabhāvapaṭilābhe nevattasañcetanā kamati, no parasañcetanā.
root_text.an4.171:7.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā sāriputto bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.171:7.2: “imassa kho ahaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa evaṁ vitthārena atthaṁ ājānāmi:
root_text.an4.171:7.3: ‘tatra, bhante, yāyaṁ attabhāvapaṭilābho yasmiṁ attabhāvapaṭilābhe attasañcetanā kamati no parasañcetanā, attasañcetanāhetu tesaṁ sattānaṁ tamhā kāyā cuti hoti.
root_text.an4.171:7.4: Tatra, bhante, yāyaṁ attabhāvapaṭilābho yasmiṁ attabhāvapaṭilābhe parasañcetanā kamati no attasañcetanā, parasañcetanāhetu tesaṁ sattānaṁ tamhā kāyā cuti hoti.
root_text.an4.171:7.5: Tatra, bhante, yāyaṁ attabhāvapaṭilābho yasmiṁ attabhāvapaṭilābhe attasañcetanā ca kamati parasañcetanā ca, attasañcetanā ca parasañcetanā ca hetu tesaṁ sattānaṁ tamhā kāyā cuti hoti.
root_text.an4.171:7.6: Tatra, bhante, yāyaṁ attabhāvapaṭilābho yasmiṁ attabhāvapaṭilābhe neva attasañcetanā kamati no parasañcetanā, katame tena devā daṭṭhabbā’”ti?
root_text.an4.171:8.1: “Ko nu kho, bhante, hetu ko paccayo, yena m’idhekacce sattā tamhā kāyā cutā āgāmino honti āgantāro itthattaṁ?
root_text.an4.171:8.2: Ko pana, bhante, hetu ko paccayo, yena m’idhekacce sattā tamhā kāyā cutā anāgāmino honti anāgantāro itthattan”ti?
root_text.an4.171:8.3: “Idha, sāriputta, ekaccassa puggalassa orambhāgiyāni saṁyojanāni appahīnāni honti, so diṭṭheva dhamme nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.171:8.4: So tadassādeti, taṁ nikāmeti, tena ca vittiṁ āpajjati;
root_text.an4.171:8.5: tattha ṭhito tadadhimutto tabbahulavihārī aparihīno kālaṁ kurumāno nevasaññānāsaññāyatanūpagānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.171:8.6: So tato cuto āgāmī hoti āgantā itthattaṁ.
root_text.an4.171:9.1: Idha pana, sāriputta, ekaccassa puggalassa orambhāgiyāni saṁyojanāni pahīnāni honti, so diṭṭheva dhamme nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.171:9.2: So tadassādeti, taṁ nikāmeti, tena ca vittiṁ āpajjati;
root_text.an4.171:9.3: tattha ṭhito tadadhimutto tabbahulavihārī aparihīno kālaṁ kurumāno nevasaññānāsaññāyatanūpagānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.171:9.4: So tato cuto anāgāmī hoti anāgantā itthattaṁ.
root_text.an4.171:10.1: Ayaṁ kho, sāriputta, hetu ayaṁ paccayo, yena m’idhekacce sattā tamhā kāyā cutā āgāmino honti āgantāro itthattaṁ.
root_text.an4.171:10.2: Ayaṁ pana, sāriputta, hetu ayaṁ paccayo, yena m’idhekacce sattā tamhā kāyā cutā anāgāmino honti anāgantāro itthattan”ti.

translation_text.an4.171:1.1: “Mendicants, as long as there’s a body, the intention that gives rise to bodily action causes pleasure and pain to arise in oneself.
translation_text.an4.171:1.2: As long as there’s a voice, the intention that gives rise to verbal action causes pleasure and pain to arise in oneself.
translation_text.an4.171:1.3: As long as there’s a mind, the intention that gives rise to mental action causes pleasure and pain to arise in oneself. But this is only when dependent on ignorance.
translation_text.an4.171:2.1: By oneself one instigates the choice that gives rise to bodily, verbal, and mental action, conditioned by which that pleasure and pain arise in oneself.
translation_text.an4.171:2.2: Or else others instigate the choice …
translation_text.an4.171:2.3: One instigates the choice after deliberation …
translation_text.an4.171:2.4: Or else one instigates the choice without deliberation …
translation_text.an4.171:5.1: Ignorance is included in all these things.
translation_text.an4.171:5.2: But when ignorance fades away and ceases with nothing left over, that body, that voice, and that mind no longer exist dependent on which that pleasure and pain arise in oneself.
translation_text.an4.171:5.3: That field does not exist, that ground does not exist, that basis does not exist, that foundation does not exist, dependent on which that pleasure and pain arise in oneself.”
translation_text.an4.171:6.1: “Mendicants, there are four kinds of incarnation.
translation_text.an4.171:6.3: There is incarnation where one’s own intention is effective, not that of others.
translation_text.an4.171:6.4: There is incarnation where the intention of others is effective, not one’s own.
translation_text.an4.171:6.5: There is incarnation where both one’s own and others’ intentions are effective.
translation_text.an4.171:6.6: There is incarnation where neither one’s own nor others’ intentions are effective.
translation_text.an4.171:6.7: These are the four kinds of incarnation.”
translation_text.an4.171:7.1: When he said this, Venerable Sāriputta said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an4.171:7.2: “Sir, this is how I understand the detailed meaning of the Buddha’s brief statement.
translation_text.an4.171:7.3: Take the case of the incarnation where one’s own intention is effective, not that of others. Those sentient beings pass away from that realm due to their own intention.
translation_text.an4.171:7.4: Take the case of the incarnation where the intention of others is effective, not one’s own. Those sentient beings pass away from that realm due to the intention of others.
translation_text.an4.171:7.5: Take the case of the incarnation where both one’s own and others’ intentions are effective. Those sentient beings pass away from that realm due to both their own and others’ intentions.
translation_text.an4.171:7.6: But sir, in the case of the incarnation where neither one’s own nor others’ intentions are effective, what kind of gods does this refer to?”
translation_text.an4.171:7.7: “Sāriputta, it refers to the gods reborn in the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception.”
translation_text.an4.171:8.1: “What is the cause, sir, what is the reason why some sentient beings pass away from that realm as returners who come back to this place,
translation_text.an4.171:8.2: while others are non-returners who don’t come back?”
translation_text.an4.171:8.3: “Sāriputta, take an individual who hasn’t given up the lower fetters. In this very life they enter and abide in the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception.
translation_text.an4.171:8.4: They enjoy it and like it and find it rewarding.
translation_text.an4.171:8.5: If they abide in that, are committed to it, and meditate on it often without losing it, when they die they’re reborn in the company of the gods of the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception.
translation_text.an4.171:8.6: When they pass away from there, they’re a returner, who comes back to this place.
translation_text.an4.171:9.1: Sāriputta, take an individual who has given up the lower fetters. In this very life they enter and abide in the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception.
translation_text.an4.171:9.2: They enjoy it and like it and find it rewarding.
translation_text.an4.171:9.3: If they abide in that, are committed to it, and meditate on it often without losing it, when they die they’re reborn in the company of the gods of the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception.
translation_text.an4.171:9.4: When they pass away from there, they’re a non-returner, not coming back to this place.
translation_text.an4.171:10.1: This is the cause, this is the reason why some sentient beings pass away from that realm as returners who come back to this place,
translation_text.an4.171:10.2: while others are non-returners who don’t come back.”

variant_text.an4.171:5.3: vatthu taṁ → vatthuṁ taṁ (bj, pts1ed); vatthuntaṁ (sya-all)
